summary: Large gm_1601 decompilation pass focused on game-mode/result/vs setup helpers, backed by header and struct typing cleanup across gm, mn, camera, audio, toy, and baselib headers. The decomp.dev report showed +1984 matched bytes, +0.05% overall matched code, 22 newly matching gm_1601 functions, and 8 additional gm_1601 functions improved but not fully matched.
what_changed: Implemented many previously stubbed gm_1601 functions, including audio balance helpers gm_801601C4, gm_80160244, gm_801602C0, gm_801603B0; victory/theme/result path lookups fn_80160400 and gm_80160438; trophy/model path helpers gm_801604DC and gm_80160564; unlockable-character mapping helpers fn_801605EC and gm_80160638; player HUD color selection gm_80160854; match/result utility helpers; camera wrapper helpers; and develop-mode stress test create/free helpers.
Added static data tables in gm_1601.c for victory themes and result animation file paths, including entries such as GmRstMCa.dat through GmRstMFe.dat and a sentinel-like character id 0x21.
Added gm_1601.static.h definitions for NUM_UNLOCKABLE_CHARACTERS, struct lbl_803B78C8_0x6, struct VictoryTheme, struct ResultAnimEntry, and the static lbl_803B78C8 unlockable-character table.
Replaced many gm_1601.h UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete signatures using u8, s8, s32, bool, f64, HSD_GObj*, MatchEnd*, Vec3*, enum CharacterKind, and enum MajorSceneKind.
Introduced enum MajorSceneKind in src/melee/gm/types.h with MJ_* values from MJ_TITLE through MJ_COUNT, including MJ_CLASSIC_GOVER, MJ_ADVENTURE_GOVER, and MJ_DEBUG_GOVER.

Improved field names in shared game/menu structs: PlayerInitData fields such as c_kind, slot_type, stocks, color, slot, spawn_dir, sub_color, handicap, team, and cpu_level; lbl_8046B6A0_t fields such as pauser, pause_timer, hud_enabled, terminate_match, is_singleplayer, match_result, match_over, frame_count, timer_seconds, FighterMatchInfo, slot_type, spawn_point, and respawn_timer; and gmm_x1CB0 fields such as item_freq, item_mask, rumble, sound_balance, deflicker, saved_language, and stage_mask.
Updated dependent code in gm_16AE.c, gm_17C0.c, gm_1B03.c, gmvsmelee.c, gmmain_lib.c, and related headers to use the newly named struct fields and typed prototypes.

Refined external prototypes needed by gm_1601 work, including Camera_8002F9E4(s8, s8), Camera_8002FC7C(u8, u8), lbAudioAx_800245F4(u8), un_8030813C(s16, enum MajorSceneKind), and hsd_80398310(s32, s32, s32, s32).
smart_moves: Converted placeholder prototypes to concrete types before or alongside implementation, which made gm_1601 code more readable and exposed reusable signatures for camera, audio, toy, and baselib calls.

Used small static lookup tables for character victory themes and result animation file paths rather than scattering hard-coded switch logic; the table shape also documents sentinel behavior around character id 0x21.
Propagated meaningful struct field names across call sites, reducing future reliance on x0/x1-style placeholders and making later matching work in gm_16AE/gm_17C0/gm_1B03 easier.
Captured major scene IDs as an enum, enabling functions such as gm_80160474, gm_801604DC, gm_80160564, and un_8030813C to express intent instead of passing raw ints.
Kept partial-progress functions in source with explicit TODO comments where struct layout or item-mask logic remained unclear, rather than pretending the decompilation was fully understood.
decomp_lessons: When a file has many adjacent stubs, broad prototype cleanup and struct typing can unlock many small 100% matches at once; this PR produced 22 new gm_1601 matches according to the bot report.
Result/game-over code often depends on shared tables and scene-kind enums; reconstructing those tables can be as important as the function bodies.
Renaming struct fields should be propagated immediately to sibling game-mode files to keep signatures and access patterns consistent.
Partial matches should retain evidence of uncertainty. gm_80167BC8 kept TODO notes around offensive/defensive handicap ratios and item-mask copying, which are useful future search anchors.
Header improvements outside the main target file are legitimate matching work when they replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with real signatures required by the decompiled call sites.
naming_conventions: Major scene enum values use MJ_* names, for example MJ_CLASSIC, MJ_ADVENTURE, MJ_CLASSIC_GOVER, MJ_ADVENTURE_GOVER, MJ_DEBUG_GOVER, and MJ_COUNT.
PlayerInitData field names moved from x0/x1/x2 style toward semantic names: c_kind, slot_type, stocks, color, slot, spawn_dir, sub_color, handicap, team, and cpu_level.

Match/global state fields in lbl_8046B6A0_t were renamed toward gameplay meaning: pauser, pause_timer, hud_enabled, terminate_match, is_singleplayer, match_result, match_over, frame_count, timer_seconds, FighterMatchInfo, slot_type, spawn_point, and respawn_timer.
Game-main preferences struct gmm_x1CB0 uses compact offset-aware names such as item_freq, item_mask, rumble, sound_balance, deflicker, saved_language, and stage_mask.
New small table structs were named for their purpose when understood: VictoryTheme and ResultAnimEntry; uncertain data kept label-derived names such as lbl_803B78C8_0x6.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma dont_inline was used around gm_801601C4 and gm_80160244, indicating inlining control was needed for matching or layout-sensitive codegen.
PAD_STACK(8) was inserted in gm_801602C0 with a comment noting a possible inlined function, preserving stack behavior for a partial match.
M2C_FIELD remained in fn_80167194 for byte offset 0xD of MatchEnd, showing a pragmatic tactic when the exact struct field was not yet modeled.

Float formulas for audio balance were written directly with constants such as 200.0f, 100.0f, 50.0f, and 127.0f, matching the original arithmetic shape closely enough to improve or match several helpers.
Static arrays with sentinel entries were used for lookup loops, including character id 0x21 and NULL/-1 terminal values.
Typed wrapper functions around camera calls preserved narrow argument sizes, for example Camera_8002F9E4(s8, s8) and Camera_8002FC7C(u8, u8).
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
decomp.dev bot reported 22 new matches in main/melee/gm/gm_1601 and 8 improvements in unmatched gm_1601 functions.
The bot listed gm_80167BC8, fn_8016719C, gm_801602C0, gm_801601C4, gm_80160244, gm_80160638, gm_80167140, and gm_80165388 as improved but not fully matched.
follow_up_queries: gm_80167BC8 item_mask TODO offensive defensive ratios
un_8030813C return struct trophy model offsets
lbAudioAx_800245F4 return type void or s32
